channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Oil Prices See Weekly Rise Amid Middle East Tensions and Limited US Measures
item.description: Oil prices continued to climb this week despite the United States issuing a 30-day waiver allowing countries to buy Russian oil and petroleum products stuck at sea. Brent crude for May went up by $1.0...

item.title: Pakistan urges action over ongoing closure of Jama Masjid in Srinagar during Ramadan
item.description: Pakistan has strongly condemned the ongoing closure of the historic Jama Masjid in Srinagar, located in Indian Illegally Occupied Jammu and Kashmir (IIOJK), on the last Friday of Ramzan, highlighting ...

item.title: Trump claims US targeted military installations on Iran's Kharg Island amid regional tensions
item.description: Former President Donald Trump asserted that the United States executed what he described as one of the most powerful bombing operations in Middle Eastern history, targeting military facilities on Iran...

item.title: Jessie Buckley Emerges as Strong Favorite to Win Best Actress Oscar for Hamnet
item.description: Irish actress Jessie Buckley is poised to make history as the potential first Irish winner of the Best Actress Oscar, with experts considering her victory nearly assured ahead of the 2026 Academy Awar...

item.title: Sailor Trapped in Gulf Shares Experiences Amid Strait of Hormuz Closure
item.description: A sailor named Wang Shang, aged 32, from Henan province in China, is currently stranded in the Gulf on a foreign vessel transporting liquefied petroleum gas. The ship cannot pass through the Strait of...

item.title: Atiku Praises Nine Senators for Defecting to ADC, Rejecting Pressure to Join APC
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar don praise the decision of some senators for the 10th National Assembly to join the African Democratic Congress (ADC) instead of the ruling All Progressives Congre...

item.title: Indian LPG Tanker Stranded Near Hormuz Amid Rising US-Iran Tensions
item.description: The situation in the Gulf has become tense as a US-Iran conflict escalates, with reports of missiles and drones being observed in the air over US positions. In the midst of this heightened security ri...

item.title: Iran conflict causes gas and raw material shortage for India’s small manufacturers
item.description: The ongoing war in Iran is starting to create challenges for India’s small manufacturers. Businesses across textiles, plastics, automobiles, and other sectors are facing disruptions due to shortages i...

item.title: U.S. Targets Iranian Military on Kharg Island, Signals Potential Threats to Oil Infrastructure
item.description: The U.S. has carried out airstrikes on military installations on Kharg Island, a key Iranian outpost in the Persian Gulf, while deliberately sparing oil infrastructure, according to former President D...

item.title: Oscar ceremony security heightened amid drone threat concerns
item.description: Conan O'Brien is set to host the 98th Academy Awards in Los Angeles at the Dolby Theatre, but the event is taking place under significantly heightened security. The FBI recently issued warnings to Cal...

item.title: Registrar Rejects Wanyonyi's Application to Register Linda Mwananchi Party Due to Slogan Conflict
item.description: The Office of the Registrar of Political Parties (ORPP) has turned down Charles Wanyonyi’s application to register a political party named Linda Mwananchi Party of Kenya. Registrar John Cox Lorionokou...

item.title: Strong Wind Advisory Issued for Windsor-Essex and Chatham-Kent with Speeds Up to 100 km/h
item.description: Residents of Windsor-Essex and Chatham-Kent are being urged to prepare for potentially hazardous wind conditions as a wind warning has been issued. Meteorologists are forecasting gusts that could reac...

item.title: Analysis shows some Melbourne inner-city units cost less to buy than rent
item.description: Recent research indicates that in certain inner-city areas of Melbourne, purchasing a unit may actually be more affordable than renting. Cotality's March housing report found that servicing a mortgage...

item.title: Boat capsizes on Lake Tanganyika, killing six Kigoma health workers on vaccination duty, three still missing
item.description: A tragic boat accident happened on Lake Tanganyika on Friday, March 13, 2026, where six health workers from Kigoma District Council lost their lives. These workers were going from Kigoma Town to Kagun...

item.title: Juleanna Glover's Emails and Meetings with Jeffrey Epstein Before His Death
item.description: A Politico investigation reveals that Juleanna Glover, a prominent Washington crisis communications consultant, maintained contact with Jeffrey Epstein over a 15-month period leading up to his death i...

item.title: Examining Congressman Bong Suntay’s Overlapping Roles in Politics and Business
item.description: Quezon City Congressman Jesus “Bong” Suntay occupies overlapping roles as a public official, transport industry leader, and founder of the fuel retail chain Cleanfuel, raising concerns about transpare...

item.title: Powee Celdran Simplifies Byzantine Empire History in an Engaging Book
item.description: Powee Celdran, known for his dynamic approach to history, has released a new book on the Byzantine Empire that makes learning about this fascinating period both accessible and enjoyable. The book serv...

item.title: The US’s Pattern of Military Intervention and Its Consequences
item.description: The article reflects critically on the United States’ repeated pattern of military intervention across the world, arguing that the nation often enters conflicts without fully considering the consequen...

item.title: JD Vance privately expressed scepticism about US military action against Iran prior to Operation Epic Fury, despite public support
item.description: According to a Politico report cited by The Independent, Vice President JD Vance privately voiced significant doubts about the United States launching military strikes against Iran in the lead-up to t...

item.title: Ongoing Tensions and Military Strikes in the Middle East on March 14, 2026
